text.title: Statistical Significance: Application Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every application of `Statistical Significance: Application` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Analysis MUST use raw counts (n1, x1, n2, x2). Headline percentages alone are forbidden — they hide sample size + variance.

rationale.source: Practical Statistics for Data Scientists; experiment-design corpus

rule.rationale: 5% vs 6% sounds meaningful but at n=20 each it's noise. Raw counts let validator compute correct confidence interval; percentages do not.

rule.statement: Sample size for each arm MUST be pre-committed via power analysis. Stopping early based on direction is forbidden (peeking inflates Type I error).

rationale.source: Sequential testing literature; Optimizely / Eppo guides

rule.rationale: Peeking 5 times during a test doubles the actual alpha rate; 'significance' at week 1 reverses by week 4. Pre-committed sample size is the only honest answer.

rule.statement: Minimum detectable effect (MDE) MUST be declared pre-test. Without MDE, an 'inconclusive' result can't distinguish 'no effect' from 'underpowered'.

rationale.source: Statistics for A/B Testing — Georgi Georgiev; Eppo docs

rule.rationale: Without MDE, a null result is meaningless. With MDE you can say 'we can rule out an effect >= X%' which is actionable; without it you can only shrug.

rule.statement: Default significance test MUST be two-sided. One-sided is allowed only with pre-registered directional hypothesis + sign-off.

rationale.source: frequentist testing convention; FDA pharma guidance applied

rule.rationale: One-sided tests halve the p-value for free — easy to abuse for 'significance'. Two-sided is the honest default; one-sided requires explicit pre-registration.

rule.statement: Report MUST include 95% confidence interval on the effect estimate, not just p-value. CI shows the practical magnitude; p-value alone is a yes/no with no size info.
rule.rationale: p<0.05 with CI [+0.1%, +0.2%] is statistically real but practically nothing. p<0.05 with CI [+5%, +25%] is meaningfully positive. Without CI, decision-maker can't distinguish.

rule.statement: When preconditions in Applies If fail OR any item in Skip If triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
